case_title: FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A. VS. GRANT LOMBARD

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT GRANT LOMBARD'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OF ACTION IS GRANTED. DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE SET ASIDE PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 473(D). DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED EVIDENCE THAT VALID SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT WAS NOT EFFECTUATED BECAUSE HE DID NOT LIVE AT THE ADDRESS WHERE THE SUBSTITUTED SERVICE OCCURRED. (DECLARATION OF GRANT LOMBARD, EX. A; COUNTY OF SAN DIEGO V. GORHAM (2010) 186 CAL.APP.4TH 1215, 1229, "ONCE PROOF IS MADE THAT THE JUDGMENT IS VOID BASED ON EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE, THE JUDGMENT IS SAID TO BE EQUALLY INEFFECTIVE AND UNENFORCEABLE AS IF THE JUDGMENT WERE VOID ON ITS FACE BECAUSE IT VIOLATES CONSTITUTIONAL DUE PROCESS.") THE CASE IS DISMISSED PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 583.250. DEFENDANT WAS NOT VALIDLY SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT WITHIN THREE YEARS OF THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF HAS ARGUED NO STATUTORY BASIS THAT WOULD ALLOW FOR AN EXTENSION OF THIS MANDATORY TIME FRAME. (CCP SEC. 583.250(B).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR # 9581

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGMENT DEBTOR LOMBARD'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED. JUDGMENT DEBTOR HAS NOT CARRIED HIS BURDEN OF SHOWING HIS EARNINGS ARE EXEMPT. (CCP 703.580.) FURTHER, LOMBARD HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF TRACING HIS EXEMPT FUNDS INTO THE LEVIED ACCOUNT. (CCP 703.080.) THE SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE IS ORDERED TO RELEASE THE $951.32 IT IS CURRENTLY HOLDING TO JUDGMENT CREDITOR TOWARDS SATISFACTION OF THE JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LISA J. ESPADA, TEL. NO. 504-6195 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT GRANT LOMBARD's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL OF ACTION is Granted. Default and default judgment are set aside pursuant to CCP sec. 473(d). Defendant has provided evidence that valid service of the summons and complaint was not effectuated because he did not live at the address where the substituted service occurred. (Declaration of Grant Lombard, Ex. A; County of San Diego v. Gorham (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1215, 1229, "once proof is made that the judgment is void based on extrinsic evidence, the judgment is said to be equally ineffective and unenforceable as if the judgment were void on its face because it violates constitutional due process.") The case is dismissed pursuant to CCP sec. 583.250. Defendant was not validly served with the summons and complaint within three years of the filing of the complaint. Plaintiff has argued no statutory basis that would allow for an extension of this mandatory time frame. (CCP sec. 583.250(b).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR # 9581 =302/ehg

Ntc Of Hearing Of Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - GRANT LOMBARD, TEL. NO. (510) 967-3197, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. Judgment Debtor Lombard's claim of exemption is denied. Judgment Debtor has not carried his burden of showing his earnings are exempt. (CCP 703.580.) Further, Lombard has not met his burden of tracing his exempt funds into the levied account. (CCP 703.080.) The San Francisco County Sheriff's Office is ordered to release the $951.32 it is currently holding to Judgment Creditor towards satisfaction of the judgment. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
